description_long: This temple structure features a stepped, square marble base with multiple tiers, leading up to a rounded, bulbous midsection. Atop the midsection sits a golden, ribbed spire crowned with a circular finial and a small pointed tip. The marble base is smooth and polished, with each tier slightly receding as it ascends, creating a sense of elevation and grandeur. The golden spire is highly reflective and contrasts sharply with the white marble below. The overall design is minimalist, with little visible ornamentation aside from the geometric forms and the metallic finish of the spire. The structure is solid and monumental, evoking the style of Buddhist stupas or chortens commonly found in Himalayan regions.
description: A stepped white marble temple with a rounded midsection and a tall, golden ribbed spire topped by a circular finial. The design is minimalist and monumental, with smooth surfaces and strong geometric forms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full stepped marble base, rounded midsection, and the golden spire with its circular finial clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, but the lighting and shadowing on the marble steps are slightly different.
description_view_2: Side view with the marble base and rounded midsection visible, highlighting the symmetry and the smooth transition between tiers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 2, with the golden spire and marble base in clear alignment.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist, smooth surfaces with geometric forms, no visible carvings or reliefs
attribute_entrance_design: No visible entrance; the structure is solid with no doorways or openings.
